# Alias Catalog Checklist: Place Alias Resolution

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of alias catalog and place-resolution requirements before task generation
**Created**: 2026-06-28
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [X] CHK001 Are all initially supported city aliases explicitly identified or bounded by reference to the existing catalog? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [X] CHK002 Are all selected Mexican postal codes and their canonical timezone mappings explicitly specified? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005]
- [X] CHK003 Are unsupported Mexican postal-code requirements defined separately from unknown alias requirements? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-011]
- [X] CHK004 Are requirements defined for using aliases and postal codes in both lookup and comparison flows? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012]
- [X] CHK005 Are requirements defined for preserving existing custom working-hours behavior when aliases or postal codes are used? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-014]

## Requirement Clarity

- [X] CHK006 Is the canonical source of truth clearly defined as IANA timezone identifiers across lookup and comparison behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [X] CHK007 Are alias normalization rules specific enough to distinguish allowed normalization from fuzzy matching? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009]
- [X] CHK008 Is the output behavior for resolved aliases and postal codes unambiguous for `Place` and `Timezone` labels? [Clarity, Spec §FR-016]
- [X] CHK009 Is the unsupported postal-code error message requirement specific enough to distinguish selected support from full postal-code coverage? [Clarity, Contract §Error Output]
- [X] CHK010 Is the meaning of "curated catalog" constrained enough to prevent inferred geocoding or remote lookup? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-007]

## Requirement Consistency

- [X] CHK011 Do the spec, data model, and CLI contract agree on the three supported Mexican postal codes and timezone mappings? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Data Model §Supported Mexican Postal Code, Contract §Supported Mexican Postal Codes]
- [X] CHK012 Do the ambiguity requirements consistently prohibit automatic timezone selection across user stories, functional requirements, and contract errors? [Consistency, Spec §US3, Spec §FR-010, Contract §Error Output]
- [X] CHK013 Do output stability requirements align with the decision to avoid new alias-specific labels? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-016, Contract §Stability Requirements]
- [X] CHK014 Do catalog validation requirements align between future-maintenance requirements and the data model validation rules? [Consistency, Spec §FR-019, Spec §FR-020, Data Model §Supported Catalog Entry]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [X] CHK015 Are success criteria measurable for all supported alias and postal-code resolution outcomes? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-002]
- [X] CHK016 Are unsupported, ambiguous, and invalid catalog states covered by measurable success criteria? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-008]
- [X] CHK017 Is behavior preservation measurable for existing IANA lookup, comparison, and working-hours commands? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005]
- [X] CHK018 Is the performance target defined in user-visible terms and tied to the existing project workflow? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]

## Scenario Coverage

- [X] CHK019 Are primary successful flows covered for alias lookup, alias comparison, postal-code lookup, and postal-code comparison? [Coverage, Spec §User Scenarios]
- [X] CHK020 Are exception flows covered for unknown aliases, unsupported postal codes, ambiguous catalog matches, and invalid working-hours precedence? [Coverage, Spec §US3, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK021 Are normalization scenarios covered for case, whitespace, punctuation separators, and accents? [Coverage, Spec §US1, Spec §FR-008]
- [X] CHK022 Are leading-zero postal-code scenarios addressed so postal codes are treated as strings? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §Supported Mexican Postal Code]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [X] CHK023 Are offline and deterministic operation requirements explicit enough to exclude external services and databases? [Dependency, Spec §FR-007, Spec §Assumptions]
- [X] CHK024 Are catalog maintenance assumptions clear enough for future additions to remain reviewable and testable? [Assumption, Spec §FR-019, Spec §FR-020]
- [X] CHK025 Are out-of-scope behaviors documented for fuzzy matching, partial search, inferred geocoding, prompts, and complete postal-code coverage? [Scope, Spec §Assumptions, Contract §Accepted Place Inputs]

## Notes

- Items should be checked only when the requirements themselves are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable.
- This checklist validates requirements quality; it does not verify implementation behavior.
